observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for the Local Road Networks following the straight line method pursuant to Paragraph 59 of IPSAS 17 and the accounting guidelines and procedures set under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Further, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the roads with complete description and classify every component to establish accuracy and correctness in the provision of depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portions of the liability as separate classifications on the face of the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with Paragraph 80 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and  provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the year as the basis of the latter in recording/book up  the  RPT and SET Receivables Accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to prepare the IIRUP for those unserviceable PPE items to be inspected and be sold through public auction if found valuable and to conduct investigation for the non-existing/missing properties and submit the results thereof to the Local Chief Executive for its final disposal
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO to update their records to come up with reconciled balances of PPE.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the payable accounts to ensure the accuracy of the balances in the financial statements and only record transactions/set up liability based on valid obligations with complete evidences or documents, and to revert the balances of Accounts Payable that have been outstanding for more than two years which are undocumented and no longer valid to the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to COA Circular No. 99-004.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant to:
Make a representation relative to the prepayments made to determine if the amounts have already been expended considering the length of time that has lapsed and prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the unexpired portion of the Other Prepayments account that were not used, if any; and
Coordinate with the City Government of Laoag and the MMMH and MC regarding the remaining balances of the deposits to be refunded and to be receipted by the Local Government Unit, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the execution of contract by the contractor in the delivery of goods in case there is a delay pursuant to Section 68 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.  Likewise, we recommended and Management also agreed to instruct the officers and employees responsible in every procurement activity to be vigilant in accomplishing the prescribed forms completely specifically on the dates portion to ascertain the authenticity of the transactions and to promote operational efficiency and effectiveness of the Municipality.
Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand payment for the Liquidated Damages of ₱24,925.00 from the contractor to be receipted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly review all supporting documents as to their propriety, regularity and accuracy specifically on the correctness of amounts.  Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to direct all heads of end-user departments to stop the practice of making erasures, alterations or tampering of amounts in the documents as these are considered forgery.

We recommended that Management through the BAC to require the winning bidders to submit the approved CHSPs duly received/acknowledged by the DOLE as evidence of the pending approval of their CSHPs, so as not to prevent Management for the issuance of the NTP.
In case the contractors will not comply on the submission of the said documents after the infrastructure projects were satisfactorily completed, this would cause Management to hold the payment of their billings.
Further, we recommended that Management require the BAC and TWG to carefully scrutinize the sufficiency of the Contractor’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded and not on an individual project especially in cases where only one bidder bids in several projects, which have the same or overlapping implementation period to avoid slippage/delay and substandard quality of work.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with all department heads responsible for the implementation of GAD PPAs to come up with strategic plans for the timely, efficient and effective implementation of these GAD PPAs in order to attain and deliver desirable benefits and outcomes to the beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management to prepare and submit Report on Physical Count of PPE for the current year 2019 to determine the actual physical count and condition of PPE at a given date including those unreconciled balances and those properties which were not be included in the report; prepare/maintain and update Property Cards and the PPE Ledger Cards by the Property Officer and Accountant to facilitate reconciliation of the PPE Account; and direct the Property Custodian to identify those unserviceable properties already subjected for disposal, then prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property which serves as basis by the Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE Account.
observations.managementAction:

The GSO already prepared IIRUP and those items were sold out and has been dropped from the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all officers and employees concerned to account and settle cash advances immediately after carrying out the purpose for which these were granted in conformity with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; Section 89 of PD 1445; immediately issue demand letters to those employees and to the members of the family of those who already passed away and no longer in the service with unsettled/ unliquidated cash advances; and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with existing cash advances unless the previous cash advances are first settled and cause the refund of any unused/unspent/unutilized cash advances and/or no longer needed to the collecting officer to be receipted and shall be deposited to the authorized government depository bank.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management comply with the system stated therein for the proper transfer of Local Road Networks to its appropriate account.
We have recommended and the management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to reconcile the balances between the subsidiary ledger and the RPCPPE to reflect the correct PPE year-end balances for fair presentation in the Financial Statement.
We have recommended and the management agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the structures built over properties not owned by the municipality.
We have recommended and the management agreed to create the necessary committees in order to facilitate the proper disposal of identified unserviceable and obsolete properties.
We have recommended the following:
The GSO-Designate and Accounting Department work together to reconcile the physical inventory count and accounting record and verify the unaccounted properties.
The management collects from the accountable officers the value of the properties that were lost and those which cannot be ascertained on its whereabouts.
Property Acknowledgement Receipt be renewed every three years or as often as necessary
We have recommended and management agreed to reclassify the proper inventory account of the tangible assets with estimated useful life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE. An Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) be prepared upon issuance of small tangible items for monitoring, control and accountability.

We have recommended that management direct the personnel under the Office of the Treasury to revisit and implement accordingly the provisions of the current Municipal Tax Ordinance. Notice of Charge will be issued to accountable officer/s for the under-assessment and/or under collection of mayor’s permit fee. Also, require the submission of the other 168 approved business for audit.

We have recommended that management require the treasury personnel to strictly implement the provision of their Municipal Tax Ordinance on the imposition of surcharges and interest on late payment of permit fees. Notice of Charge will be issued to accountable officers on the non-collection of surcharges and interest for late payment of permit fee.

We have recommended that management require the treasury personnel to be more focus and strictly implement the prescribed rates. For the improper rate of governor’s permit fee collected, the actual fee is to be established and necessary action must be enforced.
We have recommended that the collectors issue the pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence.

We have recommended and management agreed to exert efforts for the immediate liquidation/settlement of the Receivable accounts. If the probability of collection or liquidation is remote, the Municipal Accountant take responsibility to conduct a final review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to the guidelines and procedures in reconciling and cleaning the books of accounts of LGUs of dormant receivable accounts for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements provided by COA Circular 2016-005.
We have recommended that management comply with the provisions of the travel law. And the excessive disbursements should be refunded by the concerned personnel.
We have recommended for the strict observance of the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 that valid travel order be issued by proper authorities.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries of the affected accounts and be guided to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence fund.

We have recommended that the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal School Board stop the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects not included in the provisions under RA 7160 and the new DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries of the affected accounts. Immediate liquidation of the fund transferred be required if the purpose for which it has been transferred has been served.

We have recommended and the management agreed to follow the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of the funds released to NGOs/POs provided under COA Circular 2007-001 and to require the submission of documents mentioned in Items 2.1 and 2.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 in order that the municipality be able to verify the implementation of the projects as agreed upon and ensure that government funds be spent in accordance with the purposes for which the same are granted.

We have recommended that management instruct the Office of the MSWD to strengthen their evaluation process on the applicants for financial assistance so that more appropriate recipients will benefit on the AICS program.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed more to be prudent in recording government transactions such that the correct account should be used properly to avoid misperception as to the nature of the transactions when viewed by the end users of the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Secretariat of the Scholarship Committee properly observe the conditions contained in the Agreement, nonconformity thereof is ground for termination of the educational assistance. Also, unnecessary payment of educational assistance be refunded by concerned personnel.
We have recommended and the management agreed to  require the MDRRMO-Designate to submit the needed documents to ascertain the completeness and validity of the disbursement.

We have recommended that management stop receiving advance delivery of goods and strictly observe the procurement process provided in R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in order to effect the legality of the procurement and to avoid disallowances in the audit.

We have recommended that  Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the  provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations on proper notice and execution of awards on procurement in order to effect the propriety, validity and legality of the transactions.
We have recommended that BAC comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to attain transparency in the conduct of procurement.
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee give justifiable reason on their basis of the number of days to complete the project which has wide disparity on the Program of Work.

We have recommended and the management agreed to strictly follow  the provisions embodied in COA Circular No. 101C to facilitate action on papers relative to suspension orders on government infrastructure contracts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide justification as to the observed difference on the materials placed in the projects and submit the Variation or Change Order duly approved by the Local Chief Executive, including all the necessary supporting documents to support the modification.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer to provide justification on the delayed completion of the abovementioned projects and implement an efficient monitoring system for all on-going projects. Also, the Municipal Accountant is advice to impose liquidated damages.

We have recommended that management should properly observe the guidelines embodied in Annex “E” of the IRR of RA 9184. Further, the Municipal Engineer and Project Monitoring Committee should consistently monitor the infrastructure projects of the municipality to ensure its completion within the target date.

For the remaining suspension and disallowances, we have recommended that the concerned personnel and officials responsible and/or liable to immediately settle the subject of their audit disallowances in accordance with the rules and regulations on the settlement of accounts.

We have recommended that management direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to obtain the necessary documents as the basis of determining the cost of the properties to be reflected in the RPCPPE and in the books of accounts to show the correct balances of the affected asset accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the management agreed to implement measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections in order to reflect the correct cash balances in the financial statements:

Revenue collectors should be required to prepare RCD’s at the close of each business day and effect remittance of ALL their daily collections. For computerized receipts, the collectors should generate their daily collections and not by the OR series that they wanted to remit. Also, stop ante-dating the receipt of collections.
   
The liquidating officer should strengthen its verification and remittance function. Procedures for verification of receipts should be streamlined to ensure prompt recording and remittance of collections.

The Treasurer’s Office should be provided with a link/network on the computerized collection system to be utilized as added controls to ensure correctness of amounts and proper cut-off of transactions.
observations.managementAction:
The liquidating officer required the revenue collectors to also present the unused accountable forms when they remit their collections.


A link was set-up in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer
observations.reason: There are still collectors who do not remit all their collection of the day.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to attain the correctness and accuracy of the cash balance.

Also, a subsidiary ledger and file of documents for all transactions in which checks became stale be maintained separately to facilitate processing of future claims, if there will be any.

Furthermore, the records of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office  and Accounting Office be reconciled regularly to closely monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjusting entries was made as of December 31, 2016
observations.reason: Complied
